p.strong: Leader-only operation with Figure 8 safety constraint

div.p: Entries at indices < N from previous terms are now indirectly committed Example: If commitIndex advances to 10 and log[8].term < currentTerm, entry 8 is now committed

Why? An entry from a previous term replicated to majority is NOT safe to commit directly. A new leader could overwrite it. Committing a current-term entry proves this leader was elected by majority → majority acknowledged this log → previous entries are safe.
Target index must be higher than current commitIndex
Entry replicated to quorum of nodes (≥ ⌈(N+1)/2⌉)
Entry is from current term (Figure 8 fix!)
